author: Kris Oei

Merge pull request #3997 from firecrawl/feat/laravel-ai-tools

feat(php-sdk): native tool classes for the Laravel AI SDK

author: firecrawl-spring[bot]
message:
feat(api): point ACUC calls at auth_credit_usage_chunk_53 (#4012)

Switches authCreditUsageChunk / authCreditUsageChunkFromTeam to the v53 RPCs,
which auto-fall-back plan-less teams in multi-team orgs to org-wide/Scale-
Enterprise rate limits. Requires the firecrawl-db v53 migration to be applied
first.

Co-authored-by: firecrawl-spring[bot] <254786068+firecrawl-spring[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

author: firecrawl-spring[bot]
message:
chore(billing): remove the idle credit-usage tally loop (#4010)

The index-worker ran tallyBilling() every 5min: it drained the
`billed_teams` Redis set and called update_tally_10_team for each team to
aggregate credit_usage -> credit_usage_tally. #3992 removed the two sadd
feeders of `billed_teams` (Autumn is the source of truth for credits, and
the tally's readers have been migrated off it), so the set is permanently
empty and the loop is dead work.

Removes tallyBilling(), the 5-minute billingTallyInterval (+ its
clearInterval), and the now-unused updateTallyTeam wrapper in db/rpc.ts.
The update_tally_* DB functions are dropped separately in firecrawl-db.

Co-authored-by: firecrawl-spring[bot] <254786068+firecrawl-spring[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

author: Kris Oei
message:
ci(php-sdk): extract SDK_VERSION with the same permissive pattern as the version gate

The tag step's sed required exactly three numeric components, but
check_version_has_incremented.py extracts any quoted string — so a
pre-release like 1.10.0-beta.1 would pass the gate and then fail the job
before tagging and the Packagist notify. Capture whatever the gate
approved instead; the gate's version parse remains the format validator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Kris Oei
message:
docs(php-sdk): note why assertSuccess covers only synchronous endpoints

Review flagged the asymmetry: crawl start/status skip the success:false
envelope check. That is deliberate — a failed start yields a null job ID
that pollCrawl() and the crawl tool both guard, and crawl status is
surfaced explicitly on CrawlJob — but nothing said so.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: firecrawl-spring[bot]
message:
feat(billing): wire batch billing onto bill_team_7 (#4008)

bill_team_7 (firecrawl-db #214) drops bill_team_6's fetch_subscription
param and its subscription self-fetch. That self-fetch was already dead
in the live path: billTeam -> queueBillingOperation stores
`subscription_id ?? null`, so by the time supaBillTeam runs, the value is
string|null (never undefined) and `fetch_subscription: subscription_id
=== undefined` was always false. So this swap is behavior-preserving --
it just deletes the unused lookup capability.

Renames the db/rpc wrapper billTeam6 -> billTeam7 (one fewer param) and
points supaBillTeam at it. bill_team_6 stays in the DB for backward compat.

Does NOT yet retire ACUC sub_id: ~20 controllers still thread
req.acuc?.sub_id into billTeam, stamping it onto credit_usage.subscription_id.
Removing those + dropping sub_id from the ACUC is the follow-up.

Co-authored-by: firecrawl-spring[bot] <254786068+firecrawl-spring[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

author: Kris Oei
message:
ci(php-sdk): tag releases on firecrawl-php so Packagist publishes stable versions

The publish workflow force-pushed the subtree split to firecrawl-php main
and notified Packagist, but never created a version tag. Packagist derives
stable versions from tags, so the package exposed only dev-main and
constraints like ^1.9 could not resolve. Push the split as
refs/tags/v{SDK_VERSION} before the Packagist notify, skipping when the tag
already exists so workflow re-runs stay idempotent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: firecrawl-spring[bot]
message:
chore(billing): stop populating the billed_teams tally set (#3992)

billTeam/batch_billing and the ACUC cache-clear path added each team to the
Redis "billed_teams" set, which the index-worker loop drained into
update_tally_10_team (the legacy credit_usage_tally pipeline). Autumn is the
source of truth for credits now; that tally, coupons decrement, and
credit_usage.tallied are no longer authoritative. Stop feeding the set so the
loop idles. The set can only shrink from here (drained by the still-running
loop), so nothing piles up.

Removes the now-unused getRedisConnection import from auth.ts.

Co-authored-by: firecrawl-spring[bot] <254786068+firecrawl-spring[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

author: Kris Oei
message:
fix(php-sdk): address review findings on budgets, deadlines, and coverage

Truncation notices now fit inside the stated cap, a single oversized item
reduces to an omitted marker instead of bypassing the output budget, the
crawl budget counts serialized page length, crawl start and status requests
are bounded by the remaining wall-clock deadline via new optional per-request
timeouts on startCrawl/getCrawlStatus, and the FirecrawlTools pass-through
test now exercises all four tools. Documented the PHP 8.3 dev requirement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Kris Oei
message:
fix(php-sdk): use UUID-formatted crawl idempotency keys

The API's idempotency middleware only accepts UUID keys and rejects
anything else with a 409, verified against production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Kris Oei
message:
fix(php-sdk): bound crawl tool lifecycle and tool output budgets

Crawl tool now starts jobs with an idempotency key (stable across HTTP
retries), polls under one wall-clock deadline, reports pagination cursors
instead of following them, and returns a status-explicit JSON object so
failed or partial crawls are visible. Search and map outputs are capped by
a whole-result character budget with an explicit omitted marker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Kris Oei
message:
fix(php-sdk): bound crawl wait time for queue-friendly execution

FirecrawlClient::crawl() polls for up to 300 seconds by default, but
Laravel queue workers commonly kill jobs at 60 seconds, orphaning a
crawl that keeps running (and billing) on the server. Add an
overridable $timeoutSeconds (default 55) to FirecrawlCrawl, catch
JobTimeoutException in handle() to return a specific, actionable
message instead of a generic guard() error, and document the wait in
the tool description and README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Kris Oei
message:
fix(php-sdk): cap Laravel AI tool output sizes

A large scraped page or crawl result could overflow the model context
or a consumer's conversation storage. Add a shared truncate() helper
on FirecrawlTool and apply it: scrape output caps at 80000 characters,
each crawled page's markdown caps at 15000, and the map tool's limit
(both the runtime clamp and the schema max) drops from 5000 to 500.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Kris Oei
message:
fix(php-sdk): use underscore-prefixed integration tag for old API compatibility

Prefix the Laravel AI tools' integration tag with an underscore
(_laravel-ai) instead of adding a new value to the API's integration
enum. The API's integration validator accepts any underscore-prefixed
value on every deployed version, so the tools work against lagging or
self-hosted APIs without a deploy ordering requirement. Revert the
apps/api enum addition to match origin/main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Kris Oei
message:
fix(php-sdk): surface success=false API envelopes instead of empty results

The API can return HTTP 200 with a success:false envelope (e.g. DNS
failures), which the client was hydrating into an empty Document,
SearchData, or MapData instead of raising the error. Add an
assertSuccess helper and apply it to scrape, search, and map, the
synchronous endpoints the Laravel tools call directly, so failures
surface to the model instead of reading as "no content."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
